metadata.title: Domain Entity Implementation with Relational Database Design

story.iWant: create a proper Domain entity with full relational database design and link it to all existing entities
story.soThat: we establish data integrity, eliminate redundancy, enable proper foreign key relationships, and create a single source of truth for domain information
story.tasks: Phase 1: Prisma Schema Updates - Add Domain model to prisma/schema.prisma with all fields (id, company, name, logo, info, industry, note, contact, status, timestamps); Phase 2: Create Domain Module - Create directory structure, entity with TypeORM decorators, interfaces, DTOs, service with CRUD operations, controller with REST endpoints, register module in AppModule; Phase 3: Update User Module - Add ManyToOne relationship, update DTOs, update service for domain validation, update controller for domain filtering; Phase 4: Update Other Affected Modules - Find all domain_id references, add TypeORM relationships, update DTOs/services/controllers; Phase 5: Data Migration - Create migration script, populate Domain table, verify data integrity, add constraints; Phase 6: Testing Implementation - Create domain factory, write unit tests, write integration tests, verify coverage 70%+; Phase 7: Documentation and Validation - Verify Swagger docs, create seed data, update README, run linting and formatting

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
**Given** the current codebase with manually-entered domain_id UUIDs **When** we implement the Domain entity with relationships **Then** all domain_id fields must reference valid Domain records through proper foreign key constraints
**AC #1:** Phone numbers must be unique per domain (same phone allowed across different domains) **AC #2:** Domain CRUD operations must be available through API **AC #3:** All existing tests must continue to pass **AC #4:** Migration must successfully convert existing domain_id values **AC #5:** Test coverage must remain at 70%+ **AC #6:** Users can be created/updated with proper domain validation

doc.title: Domain Entity Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Currently, the domain_id field exists in multiple tables as a manually-entered UUID string without any database-level constraints, relationships, or validation. Implement a proper Domain entity with full relational database design.

doc.title: Boilerplate Product Brief
doc.snippet: Boilerplate is a production-ready, enterprise-grade NestJS boilerplate for all company backend projects. Reduces new project setup from 1 week to 1 day.

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: Technical Type: API/Backend Framework (Boilerplate). This project is a backend API boilerplate positioned as an internal developer productivity tool.

doc.title: Project Documentation
doc.snippet: This project uses environment variables for configuration. Configure database, JWT, AWS S3, SendGrid, Redis, and other services via .env file.

doc.title: System Architecture
doc.snippet: NestJS-based backend with modular architecture, Prisma ORM, PostgreSQL/MongoDB support, comprehensive testing infrastructure.

file.reason: Database schema definition - add Domain model here
ManyToOne relationship example to follow for Domain entity

story-context.constraints: - Must follow Controller-Service-Repository layered architecture pattern - Use TypeORM decorators: @Entity, @PrimaryGeneratedColumn('uuid'), @ManyToOne, @JoinColumn, @Column, @CreateDateColumn, @UpdateDateColumn, @DeleteDateColumn - Follow 8-category import organization: Libraries → DTOs → Services → Repositories → Entities → Interfaces → Enums → Events - Use path aliases: @/ maps to src/ for absolute imports - Domain entity must support soft delete via @DeleteDateColumn - ManyToOne relationship: onDelete SET NULL, onUpdate CASCADE - Phone number uniqueness constraint: (phone_number, domain_id) UNIQUE - JSONB columns for flexible data: info (CompanyInfo), contact (ContactInfo) - Status enum: ACTIVE = 1, PASSIVE = 0 - Test coverage must maintain 70%+ threshold - All new code must have corresponding unit tests - Must use Faker.js for test data generation - Follow existing controller-service-repository test patterns

interface.name: CompanyInfo
interface.signature: interface CompanyInfo { vat?: string; tax_number?: string; address?: string; email?: string; phone_number?: string; website?: string; }

interface.name: ContactInfo
interface.signature: interface ContactInfo { first_name?: string; last_name?: string; phone_number?: string; email?: string; title?: string; }

interface.name: Domain
interface.signature: class Domain { id: string; company: string; name: string; logo: string; info?: CompanyInfo; industry?: string; note?: string; contact?: ContactInfo; status: StatusEnum; created_at: Date; updated_at: Date; deleted_at?: Date | null; }

interface.name: CreateDomainDto
interface.signature: class CreateDomainDto { company: string; name: string; logo: string; info?: CompanyInfo; industry?: string; note?: string; contact?: ContactInfo; }

interface.name: UpdateDomainDto
interface.signature: class UpdateDomainDto { company?: string; name?: string; logo?: string; info?: CompanyInfo; industry?: string; note?: string; contact?: ContactInfo; status?: StatusEnum; }

tests.standards: Testing uses Jest 30.0.0 with @nestjs/testing utilities. Test files follow pattern: *.spec.ts for unit tests, *.integration.spec.ts for integration tests. Coverage threshold: 70% minimum, common modules 100%, service classes 80%. Must use Faker.js for test data generation. Test structure follows controller-service-repository pattern with beforeEach setup. Mock repositories using getRepositoryToken(). Integration tests use Test.createTestingModule().
tests.locations: Unit Tests: test/modules/domain/domain.service.spec.ts, test/modules/domain/domain.controller.spec.ts Integration Tests: test/modules/domain/domain.integration.spec.ts Test Factories: test/modules/domain/factories/domain.factory.ts Global Tests: test/jest-setup.ts, test/factories/
tests.ideas: - Test DomainService.create() with valid data (AC #2) - Test DomainService.findOne() with existing/non-existing IDs (AC #2) - Test DomainService.update() with partial updates (AC #2) - Test UserService.createUser() requires valid domain (AC #6) - Test phone uniqueness per domain constraint (AC #1) - Test migration script converts existing domain_id values (AC #4) - Test all existing tests still pass after changes (AC #3) - Test test coverage remains above 70% (AC #5) - Test Domain CRUD API endpoints return correct responses (AC #2) - Test soft delete on Domain entity
